mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 7.5% signals improving labor market conditions, which boost corporate earnings visibility and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with UNRATE < 7.5% fall below 0.5% on average, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns drops below 0.25.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) signals strong real economic activity, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate profitability.
failure_condition: The joint growth of INDPRO and GDPC1 fails to produce positive 60-day SPX returns in > 40% of instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 850,000 units signals strong demand for residential construction, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through sectoral spillovers.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods with HOUST > 850,000 fall below 0.8% on average, or the signal's predictive power declines to an IC < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in the CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% signals inflationary pressures that force central banks to tighten policy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with CPIAUCSL > 2.5% remain positive or flat, or the signal's IC drops below -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) below 100,000 indicates weak labor demand, which reduces consumer spending confidence and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in periods with PAYEMS < 100,000 exceed -1.5% on average, or the signal's IC becomes positive.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales (RSAFS) and GDP (GDPC1) indicates weak consumer demand, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through sectoral earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and GDPC1 fails to produce negative 20-day SPX returns in > 40% of instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) below 95.0 signals manufacturing weakness, which reduces corporate margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with INDPRO < 95.0 remain positive or flat, or the signal's IC drops below -0.05.
